rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC VS. BRIGANTINE RENDEZVOUS, INC.,A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Amend The Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 23, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC'S Motion To Amend The Judgment. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ASSET ACCEPTANCE LLC VS. EMMALOU GONZALES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Asset Acceptance Llc'S Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 23, 2012, Line 18, Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. The claim will be granted if the judgment debtor can provide documentary evidence at the hearing of her monthly income and expenses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE W. THAYER VS. TRINITY PROPERTIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Ernest Thayer'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 23, 2012, Line 17, Plaintiff Ernest Thayer'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Halperin as follows: Denied. The discovery sought by Plaintiffs is unwarranted, and does not appear to be necessary for a class certification motion. Plaintiffs are not seeking additional limited discovery to create an argument for class treatment of Plaintiffs' claims based upon a statistical sample of a portion of the class. Rather, Plaintiffs seek instead to obtain individualized evidence from every single member of Plaintiffs' proposed class. The notion that a court must allow a plaintiff to obtain discovery concerning each member of a class's individual claims in order to certify a class is not supported by any case law cited by Plaintiffs. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE W. THAYER VS. TRINITY PROPERTIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Trinity Management Services And Trinity Properties'S Motion For Protective Order Re Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 23, 2012, Line 17, Defendants Trinity Management Services And Trinity Properties's Motion For Protective Order Re Discovery. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Halperin as follows: Granted. The discovery sought by Plaintiffs is unwarranted, and does not appear to be necessary for a class certification motion. Plaintiffs are not seeking additional limited discovery to create an argument for class treatment of Plaintiffs' claims based upon a statistical sample of a portion of the class. Rather, Plaintiffs seek instead to obtain individualized evidence from every single member of Plaintiffs' proposed class. The notion that a court must allow a plaintiff to obtain discovery concerning each member of a class's individual claims in order to certify a class is not supported by any case law cited by Plaintiffs. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 23, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF SCHAPIRO-THORN INC.'S Motion For An Order To Compel Defendantt To Pay Arbitration Fees. Granted. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TONY D'AMATO VS. YEVA, INC.DBA SAXE MORTGAGE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 23, 2012, Line 4, COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TONY D'AMATO'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Deny without prejudice. CRC 3.1362 requires that Judicial Council forms must be used for a notice of motion and motion to be relieved as counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER THOMSEN VS. ROCKET LAWYER INCORPORATED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 23, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANTS ROCKET LAWYER INCORPORATED and ACENDI INTERACTIVE COMPANY, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff's First and Second Causes of Action are dismissed as a matter of law on the grounds that plaintiff was properly classified as an exempt employee. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL BOSCHETTO VS. VOSS BORETA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers, Without Objections, From David Frear To Request For Admissions, Set One, Request For Sanctions Against Deft And His Counsel In The Sum Of $1987.50; Memo Of P&A; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 23, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PAUL BOSCHETTO Motion To Compel Further Answers, Without Objections, From David Frear To Request For Admissions, Set One, Request For Sanctions in theSum Of $1987.50;Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. As to all ten separate motions, counsel are directed to meet and confer in person prior to the hearing to further review, discuss and eliminate all unnecessary elements of these motions. Counsel are to appear as scheduled for the hearing and report on the progress they have made in narrowing the items in dispute. To accommodate counsel, the court will place this matter at the end of the calendar, if necessary, in order to permit the conclusion of negotiations. discussed in the separate motions (including sanctions) until the parties are prepared to give the court a full report of the disputed matters,and the resolution the parties have reached as to each disputed item.

rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE JOHNSON VS. MILENA TALIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Pltf'S Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 23, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JACQUELINE JOHNSON Motion To Compel Responses To Pltf'S Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Production Of Documents;Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied, for failure to file and serve a separate statement of the discovery matters in dispute. Sanctions are denied.Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: WELLS FARGO ADVISORS, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For A Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 23, 2012, Line 10, Plaintiff's Motion For A Preliminary Injunction. Denied. Plaintiff is unlikely to prevail on the merits. Plaintiff has not established any basis to enjoin the arbitration from proceeding. Brennan v Tremco Incorporated (2001) 25 Cal.4th 310, 314 is distinguishable. Plaintiff has not presented any evidence to demonstrate that the federal action ( A.G. Edwards & Sons, Inc. v. Nielsen (Action No. C07-02185)) started in court and ended in contractual arbitration. Further, the federal action and underlying arbitration action were pursued concurrently by Plaintiff. Plaintiff has not established that proceeding with the arbitration would result in irreparable harm to it. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CARSON PELLANDA, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL VS. GUITAR CENTER, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 23, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CARSON PELLANDA, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance With Court OrderPro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. Sanctions are denied.Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MURRAY et al VS. CHRYSTINE M LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 23, 2012, Line 11, DEFENDANT RELIABLE CAREGIVERS INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained. The demurrer to the first and second causes of action are sustained without leave to amend as Plaintiffs elected not to oppose the demurrer to these causes of action. The demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege they relied on Defendant's statements. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: BRENDA HUGHES VS. PHILIP MORRIS USA, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defednant R.J. Reynolds Tobacco Company'S Motion To Quash Notices Of Taking Depositions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 23, 2012, Line 7,Defednant R.J. Reynolds Tobacco Company'S Motion To Quash Notices Of Taking DepositionsPro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted, without prejudice to the right of the parties to reach agreement on discovery protocols which will eliminate duplicative and unnecessarily overbroad discovery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed.
